settings.text:
Every inquiry is read by a human — usually the studio manager — and routed to the artist whose style fits what you described. You'll hear back within three business days, with either a consultation time or an honest note that we're not the right fit.

No phone calls, no DMs for booking. Email is the fastest way in.

settings.title: Which artist
settings.description: If you know whose style you want, name them. If you're not sure, describe what you're going for and we'll route it.

settings.title: Size and placement
settings.description: Roughly how big, roughly where on the body. A napkin sketch photographed with your phone is fine.

settings.title: Reference images
settings.description: Two to five images that point at the direction, vibe, or subject. Other artists' work is fine as a reference — we won't copy it.

settings.title: Date window
settings.description: When you'd hope to book — a window like "July–September" is more useful than a specific date.

settings.title: Anything else
settings.description: Meaning behind the piece, constraints, existing tattoos in the area, anything that might help the artist draw the right thing.

settings.text: The process is deliberate. A human reads every inquiry, makes a call on which artist should take it, and then the artist drafts a plan before the first session. No auto-replies, no chatbot in the middle.

settings.features:
+ A human reads every inquiry — usually the studio manager
+ We route it to the right artist, and tell you who
+ The artist replies with a plan, timeline, and deposit details

settings.title: More detail on the Visit page
settings.text: The full FAQ covers pricing, deposits, licensing, aftercare, cover-ups, and the bits most people want to know before booking.

settings.question: How long until we hear back?
settings.answer: Within three business days, almost always. If the artist you named is on deep backlog it may be longer — we'll tell you upfront rather than leaving you in silence.

settings.question: What if our timing doesn't work?
settings.answer: We'll say so honestly and often suggest a different artist with earlier availability or point you to a trusted studio elsewhere in the city. A good referral is better than a vague "maybe next year."

settings.question: Can we come in for a consult without emailing first?
settings.answer: We don't take drop-in consultations — it disrupts another client's session. The written inquiry gives the artist time to think before the call, which is worth the wait.

settings.question: Do you tattoo existing clients differently?
settings.answer: Returning clients don't skip the inquiry flow — every project gets a fresh consultation. But their artists usually have them scheduled faster than first-timers, and deposits are often waived.
